skill.name: parallel-agents
skill.description: Guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code users through native multi-agent orchestration patterns for analysis, implementation review, and synthesis.
skill.summary: Coordinate specialized AI agents for multi-perspective code analysis and planning.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static external-command and weak-cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline agent names, and normal documentation text. The skill contains no executable script, network call, credential access, or prompt-injection text, but it includes dual-use security-audit and penetration-testing orchestration guidance that should be used only on authorized systems.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick matches occur inside Markdown fenced examples and a table of agent names, not executable Ruby, shell, or script code. The file is a single documentation file and the front matter only declares Read, Glob, and Grep tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched locations are visible Markdown examples or tables, and no runnable command wrapper is present. There is no user-controlled command execution path in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak-cryptography findings point to the skill description and an OpenAPI agent table row. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic implementation appears at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive prose and an API design row, not cryptographic code. No evidence found for MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: Dual-Use Security Testing Guidance
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for minor marketplace risk. The skill recommends security-auditor and penetration-tester agents for audits and active vulnerability testing. This is legitimate for authorized assessments, but the marketplace listing should make the authorization boundary clear.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text explicitly names penetration testing and exploit-oriented trigger phrases, but provides no exploit instructions or automation. The concern is usage context, not malicious implementation.

content.value_statement: Complex engineering work often needs security, backend, frontend, testing, and planning perspectives. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code users repeatable patterns for delegating work to specialized agents and synthesizing results.
actual_capabilities: Explains when multi-agent orchestration is useful and when a single agent is enough.
Provides prompt patterns for single-agent, sequential, context-passing, and resume workflows.
Lists specialized agent roles for security, backend, frontend, testing, DevOps, data, and documentation work.
Defines orchestration patterns for comprehensive analysis, feature review, and security audit workflows.
Provides a synthesis template for combining agent findings into one prioritized report.
limitations: Does not create or install agents by itself.
Does not execute code, run tests, or modify project files.
Depends on the host tool supporting named agents or equivalent delegation.
Security testing workflows require explicit authorization for the target system.

use_cases.title: Review a Feature Across Domains
use_cases.description: Coordinate backend, frontend, and test agents to inspect one change from multiple engineering viewpoints.

use_cases.title: Plan a Comprehensive Codebase Audit
use_cases.description: Use discovery, security, performance, and testing agents to map risks before larger refactoring work.

use_cases.title: Synthesize Specialist Findings
use_cases.description: Convert separate agent observations into one concise summary with priorities and action items.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Simple Specialist Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security-auditor agent to review the authentication flow. Summarize risks, evidence, and recommended fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Chain Discovery and Review
prompt_templates.prompt: First use the explorer-agent to map the project structure. Then use the backend-specialist to review API endpoints based on that map. Finish with a short synthesis.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate a Feature Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this feature with the frontend-specialist, backend-specialist, and test-engineer agents. Ask each agent for findings, then combine the results into prioritized recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Full Engineering Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Orchestrate explorer-agent, security-auditor, performance-optimizer, database-architect, and test-engineer reviews. Pass relevant findings between agents and produce one final action plan.

output_examples.input: Review a new checkout feature with multiple agents.
output_examples.output: A consolidated review that separates payment risk, API behavior, UI issues, and missing tests into prioritized action items.

output_examples.input: Audit a service before a migration.
output_examples.output: A synthesis report with architecture observations, security concerns, performance bottlenecks, database migration risks, and verification steps.
A continuation plan that references previous findings, assigns follow-up work to the right specialist, and updates the final recommendation list.

best_practices: Start with discovery before assigning specialist agents on unfamiliar projects.
Pass only relevant findings between agents to keep each review focused.
End with one synthesized report that ranks issues by impact and urgency.
anti_patterns: Do not use multi-agent orchestration for small single-file fixes.
Do not run security testing against systems without explicit authorization.
Do not publish separate agent reports without resolving conflicts and duplicates.

faq.question: Does this skill run parallel processes?
faq.answer: No. It describes orchestration prompts for AI agents inside supported tools.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does it require external scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guide and does not include scripts or executable code.

faq.question: Can it modify project files?
faq.answer: The skill itself cannot modify files. Any edits depend on the host agent and user approval.

faq.question: When should I avoid using it?
faq.answer: Avoid it for simple tasks where one agent can inspect and solve the problem directly.

faq.question: Is the security audit workflow safe to use?
faq.answer: Use it only on systems you own or have permission to test. The skill does not provide exploit code.
